value_picks.name: PG&E Corporation
value_picks.why_undervalued: Utility still discounted after years of wildfire liabilities and heavy capex; shares were near the top of the 52-week range but still a recovery setup tied to improving regulatory/risk framework.
value_picks.bull_case: If CA wildfire legislation and risk-mitigation investments continue to reduce liability overhang, earnings visibility and allowed ROE could improve, enabling multiple expansion and steady EPS growth.

value_picks.name: The Kroger Co.
value_picks.why_undervalued: Defensive grocer trading below prior-year highs amid margin and labor-cost concerns despite resilient demand and scale advantages.
value_picks.bull_case: Normalization in shrink/food inflation and execution on loyalty/retail media can lift margins, supporting buybacks and mid-single-digit EPS growth.

value_picks.name: Copart, Inc.
value_picks.why_undervalued: Shares traded near 52-week lows after a growth scare, despite an asset-light model and continued strong free-cash-flow generation.
value_picks.bull_case: If salvage volumes revert (weather comps normalize) and international growth continues, Copart can re-rate back toward historical multiples with high-ROIC compounding.

value_picks.name: Old National Bancorp
value_picks.why_undervalued: Regional bank priced at a discount to intrinsic value estimates amid lingering funding-cost and credit-cycle fears.
value_picks.bull_case: As rates stabilize/cut and deposit betas ease, NIM can recover while credit remains manageable, driving a re-rating toward historical P/TBV.

value_picks.name: Mobileye Global Inc.
value_picks.why_undervalued: Autonomous-driving supplier trading well below estimated intrinsic value after auto-cycle and ADAS adoption concerns.
value_picks.bull_case: If OEM design wins translate into renewed revenue growth and operating leverage, sentiment can reverse and the stock can recover meaningfully from depressed levels.

momentum_picks.name: Generac Holdings Inc.
momentum_picks.catalyst: Reacceleration narrative in home standby generators and grid resiliency demand; shares were among early-2026 S&P 500 leaders.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Momentum is strong, but cyclicality and any demand slowdown could drive sharp multiple compression.

momentum_picks.name: Corning Incorporated
momentum_picks.catalyst: Improving optical/AI data-center connectivity demand and cyclical recovery in display/industrial end markets supported a strong multi-month run.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Upside depends on sustained capex by hyperscalers and end-market recovery; a slowdown would likely stall gains.

momentum_picks.name: Teradyne, Inc.
momentum_picks.catalyst: Semiconductor test demand improved alongside AI/compute buildout and expectations for cycle recovery.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: High sensitivity to semiconductor capex cycles creates drawdown risk if orders roll over.

momentum_picks.name: Western Digital Corporation
momentum_picks.catalyst: Memory/storage cycle recovery and AI-driven data-center demand pushed the stock higher into mid-February 2026.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Storage pricing is volatile; any reversal in NAND/HDD pricing would hit earnings and momentum quickly.

momentum_picks.name: Moderna, Inc.
momentum_picks.catalyst: Pipeline optimism and renewed interest in biotech leaders drove a strong early-2026 rally.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Binary clinical/regulatory outcomes mean gains can reverse abruptly on adverse data.

watchlist_updates.detail: Cisco shares fell 12.3% after issuing weak guidance for the current year due to rising memory-chip prices impacting profitability.
Applied Materials surged 11% after reporting Q4 earnings of $2.30 per share and $7.01B revenue, beating expectations.
Rivian jumped 20% after announcing strong vehicle delivery projections for 2026.
CBRE plummeted 8.8% amid fears of AI disruption in real estate.
C.H. Robinson tumbled 14.5% due to AI disruption concerns in logistics.
Morgan Stanley declined 4.9% on concerns AI could hamper wealth management.
Amazon remained in bear market territory after earnings miss and tech rotation.
Booking Holdings hit new 52-week low of $4071 amid broader market rotation out of tech.
